111067.explanation: LPB is in favor of more diversity, but primarily opts for quality and local representation, not a strict 50/50 quota.

111068.explanation: Citizen participation is fundamental; residents must be able to directly influence major decisions.

111071.explanation: LPB strongly supports agricultural side activities, but never “always and everywhere”: spatial quality and livability require tailored solutions and conditions.

111072.explanation: LPB believes this does not require financial support from the municipality. The municipality can help by taking a coordinating role: bringing parties together, supporting initiatives, and ensuring local energy projects get off the ground properly. This way, LPB encourages village-oriented energy cooperatives.

111075.explanation: Scattered, small-scale, decent housing on or near the farmstead fits with LPB; employers bear responsibility for this.

111079.explanation: Joint celebrations are valuable and bring people together, but the municipality does not have to organize these themselves. Residents, associations, and village councils can do this perfectly well on their own, with municipal assistance if needed. This keeps control in the villages and the municipality supports only where necessary.

111080.explanation: Bulky waste can be disposed of free of charge to prevent dumping. Those who want bulky waste collected at home contribute fairly to the costs. This keeps the services affordable and the towns clean.

111083.explanation: Within clear frameworks and distribution, this can help with appropriate housing construction and business succession without displacing agriculture.

111084.explanation: Sport is important and money should not be a barrier. The municipality does not need to pay fees itself; LPB refers to the Youth Fund for Sport & Culture, which supports children from low-income families, including with sports equipment.

111085.explanation: School buildings are the heart. Note that one school building per village is sufficient. In cases where there are multiple schools in a village, only money should be invested in the public school.

111087.explanation: LPB believes the municipality should structurally support active sports clubs to maintain their continuity and the livability of the villages.

111088.explanation: LPB values heritage but sees that current monument care is too costly and not always efficient. Therefore, we support targeted budget cuts, provided these are accompanied by sharper priorities and more efficient implementation.

111089.explanation: LPB strives for one strong, central library because this guarantees quality, efficiency, and a broader offer for all residents.

111091.explanation: LPB believes extra supervision is necessary and therefore opts for structural deployment, good accessibility, and visible presence of BOA officers. Camera footage often helps little in practice to prevent nuisance and vandalism.

111092.explanation: Protection against domestic violence is urgently needed. The current regional approach provides good shelter and assistance. LPB does not want to change this.

111094.explanation: As long as spatial planning, recreation, and village character are not compromised.

111096.explanation: The municipality should stop debt forgiveness because the system is unfair and costly; everyone contributes according to their means and support can be more targeted through existing income schemes.

111097.explanation: Raising the income limit has little effect: it only reaches a small additional group of households while the budget becomes fragmented. Therefore, LPB believes poverty aid should remain targeted to those who really need it.

111099.explanation: Fits the role as an energy municipality and a practical approach to climate policy.

111101.explanation: Due to severe staff shortages, increasing waiting lists, and limited capacity, care must be organized as efficiently as possible. Separate care streams lead to fragmentation, higher costs, and extra pressure on an already heavily burdened care system.

111103.explanation: Property tax (OZB) is not high in Borsele. Compare with other municipalities. Keep monitoring neighboring municipalities to remain comparable in costs.

111106.explanation: LPB wants extra space for local entrepreneurs to prevent spillover to other municipalities.

111107.explanation: LPB opts for local consideration and customization; space for limited/consulted Sunday openings is desirable.

111110.explanation: Agriculture is an economic pillar; energy generation should mainly take place on roofs and other suitable locations, not at the expense of agricultural land.

111113.explanation: In a water-rich province, swimming skills are essential; school swimming promotes safety and equal opportunities.

111115.explanation: LPB finds a balanced housing mix important, but 40% social housing does not fit the scale of our villages. A maximum of about 28% better matches local needs and retains space for starters, seniors, and other small-scale housing forms that strengthen the vitality of small communities.

111119.explanation: LPB wants small-scale, scattered housing and no overconcentration in small villages; larger villages and suburban areas are more logical.

111120.explanation: Borsele is indeed an energy hub. LPB is open to nuclear energy (including new technologies such as SMRs) provided it is safe, supported by public opinion, and especially offers local benefits.

111121.explanation: LPB wants to build additional homes in all 15 villages, at least one “new street” per village, with tailored solutions per village.

111067.explanation: More important than the distribution of men/women is a board that builds trust and involvement together with our residents.

111068.explanation: We believe participation must be realistic, without raising false expectations. Roles and responsibilities must be clear. A referendum does not contribute to a transparent decision-making process.

111071.explanation: Farms are an essential part of our landscape. To keep farming (economically) healthy, a side activity can be necessary or desirable. A shop and a mini-campsite are good examples.

111072.explanation: Collective energy generation is a complex process. The municipality can help (facilitate) to find the right solution, although it is questionable whether financial support is the right tool.

111075.explanation: Farmers are generally capable of housing seasonal workers on their own property, provided there are sufficient (legal) possibilities. In addition, alternatives should be sought, excluding regular homes.

111079.explanation: If this could be an alternative to (illegal) fireworks, we think it is a good idea to explore further. But given the (still) absence of national legislation and the significant enforcement effort needed regarding (illegal) fireworks, we have our doubts.

111080.explanation: Based on the idea that “nothing is free,” we believe bulky waste should be able to be delivered simply without having to travel too far. Organizing a separate “bulky waste route” is very costly and therefore makes waste collection more expensive.

111083.explanation: The openness of our landscape should be preserved as much as possible. We must therefore limit “paving.” We build more homes near the villages.

111084.explanation: Sports and exercise are good for everyone. If the opportunity to do so is limited by insufficient financial means, we have no objection to a (partial) compensation.

111085.explanation: Our school buildings must be well and sustainably maintained. An exception for us are school buildings that have no future perspective due to an insufficient number of students.

111087.explanation: Among other things, due to a declining number of members, sports clubs can get into (financial) trouble. The municipality can help to search for the right solution. It must then be carefully considered whether a financial contribution is the best solution.

111088.explanation: Cultural heritage deserves to be preserved. Therefore, we advocate a solid monument vision and policy, of which targeted subsidies are a part.

111089.explanation: Currently, we have physical library facilities in both 's-Gravenpolder and Heinkenszand. And we want to keep it that way!

111091.explanation: Camera surveillance should only be used when it is truly necessary. Beforehand, all stakeholders must look for other solutions. Residents can feel their privacy is limited by cameras.

111092.explanation: If there is a stay-away-from-my-man shelter nearby where our residents can go, we do not see the need for an additional shelter.

111094.explanation: Our recreational parks are intended for holidaymakers and visitors recreationally, not for permanent residence. For that, we need to build extra homes near the villages.

111096.explanation: The ever-increasing costs for households certainly make it harder for people with low incomes to make ends meet. (Partial) cancellation of taxes must therefore remain possible.

111097.explanation: Due to rising costs, more and more households are approaching the poverty line. Raising the income limit certainly does not solve the problem.

111099.explanation: We advocate a policy where the supply of charging points follows the demand. No one wants unused charging points in our scarce parking spaces.

111101.explanation: We choose as a principle that care should be close by, humane, and tailored to the personal situation of residents. Free choice of a quality care provider fits perfectly within this principle.

111103.explanation: Local taxes (including property tax) must remain as low as possible. Therefore, we must clearly explain to residents where the money goes and why.

111106.explanation: A thorough study must be conducted into the need for business parks in Borsele. Equally important is the question of suitable locations.

111107.explanation: Sunday rest deserves protection. This day is a day of rest and reflection. We want to preserve Sunday as a blessing for people and society.

111110.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce, so we must think very carefully before giving this land another function. Making agricultural land available for collective energy generation can, in our view, be an option.

111113.explanation: We find it very important that children can swim. But given many challenges (transport, school hours, etc.), we do not consider it responsible to reintroduce school swimming.

111115.explanation: The agreement in the Housing Deal to strive for 30% social rental housing is already a very big challenge.

111119.explanation: We consider agricultural workers on the farmers' own land a good solution. We also think of larger existing buildings that have lost their original function (including shops, churches, and farms). New locations outside the built-up area are also an option for us.

111120.explanation: Nuclear energy can contribute to the “energy mix” needed to meet electricity demand. If nuclear power plants are built in Borssele, the “Borssele Conditions” must be met.

111121.explanation: Housing must be built in all villages. In some villages this means an entire neighborhood, in others 'just an extra street'.

111067.explanation: As CDA, we believe that everyone must be well represented in the college, young and old, male and female, all villages. Simply saying that there must be as many men as women in the college does a disservice to others.

111068.explanation: The municipal council is elected by the residents of Borsele to make considerations and decisions. However, we do believe that residents must be well involved in the decision-making process.

111071.explanation: Not always, but under certain conditions there are certainly possibilities.

111075.explanation: We do encourage this but do not enforce it.

111079.explanation: If an initiative arises, we want to support it, but we do not see this as a task for the municipality.

111083.explanation: We align with provincial regulations here.

111085.explanation: This has already been initiated and will happen even more in the coming period.

111087.explanation: Provided the vitality of the association remains guaranteed.

111089.explanation: We hold on to our two libraries because both serve important functions.

111091.explanation: This has already been initiated at several locations. Where necessary, we will assess whether further expansion is possible.

111097.explanation: In many schemes, we already apply the 130% limit.

111099.explanation: The expectation is that there will be more electric cars. Consequently, proportionally more electric charging points will be needed.

111106.explanation: We investigate how much space is needed, where possibilities exist, and how we can revitalize existing business parks. We continue to provide space for SMEs.

111107.explanation: Maintain existing policy (12 shopping Sundays per year).

111110.explanation: Agricultural land is for growing vegetables and fruit. Solar panels do not belong there.

111115.explanation: We adhere to the national standard of 30%.

111119.explanation: Housing must take place close to where the need exists. However, attention must be paid to the livability of the villages.

111120.explanation: As a municipality, we do not decide on the arrival of new nuclear power plants. For example, we have the Borsel Conditions regarding the arrival of nuclear power plants.

111121.explanation: Construction must be possible in all villages in Borsele. Therefore, we will continue working on drawing up plans for our villages.

111067.explanation: For us, it is important that a stable and strong executive board is formed.

111068.explanation: The PvdA/GL is also positive about referenda, especially as an instrument to bridge the gap between citizens and politics.

111071.explanation: In Borsele, it is already permitted to start a mini-campsite as a secondary activity related to a shop; that should also be possible.

111072.explanation: To better respond to local needs, the municipality strives to increase citizen participation, for example in improving the living environment. Citizens get involved in green initiatives, energy cooperatives, litter cleanup actions, help for the elderly and care initiatives.

111075.explanation: Labor migrants make an important contribution to the economy of Borsele. The municipality already enables labor migrants to be housed at agricultural and fruit-growing companies. We must continue to facilitate that.

111079.explanation: We believe that community spirit in the villages must come from the bottom up. The municipality does not need to organize a fireworks show but can support good ideas from residents that contribute to a pleasant New Year's Eve.

111080.explanation: Deposit points for bulky waste must be well facilitated and sufficient.

111083.explanation: We find that this is currently adequately regulated in our environmental vision.

111084.explanation: The law does not allow the municipality to pay this directly. However, there are schemes such as the sports and culture fund. In our opinion, this is well arranged in our municipality now.

111085.explanation: The PvdA/GL believes that all municipal buildings must be made sustainable.

111087.explanation: It is important to get and keep our residents healthy and active. Sport must above all be fun, which is why PvdA-GroenLinks chooses sport that is accessible to everyone, also physically.

111088.explanation: Our municipality has 173 national monuments, two protected village views, and 39 municipal monuments. Wherever possible, we make them worth visiting and explore opportunities to organize cultural events there.

111089.explanation: Borsele has 2 libraries that meet the need. As far as we are concerned, we maintain that and try to facilitate reading points in village community centers.

111091.explanation: Safety is very important for a pleasant neighborhood and a good society. But it starts with improving social cohesion, so not necessarily with more camera surveillance, unless.

111092.explanation: In Zeeland, we have arranged this regionally, and women can turn to Fier Zeeland or Veilig Thuis Zeeland.

111094.explanation: PvdAGL believes there must be a clear distinction between a holiday home and a fixed residential address.

111096.explanation: The remission of municipal taxes is an important instrument to help residents with low incomes and to prevent financial problems and debts.

111097.explanation: In Borsele, the income limit is set at 130% of the Participation Act. Raising this limit further can lead to an income trap, where extra work does not always pay off. We find that undesirable.

111099.explanation: All villages will get sufficient charging stations, such as at every new construction project. Together with residents, we examine where electric shared cars are needed. In cooperation with residents, municipality, and companies, we arrange purchase and operation.

111101.explanation: We believe that the quality of care should come first. The right care must not depend on someone's faith, culture, or background.

111103.explanation: It is important that the burdens on our residents remain as low as possible. We believe in the principle that the strongest shoulders carry the heaviest burdens. Those who have more contribute more. This ensures a fair distribution of costs within our municipality.

111106.explanation: PvdA/GL is against the expansion of industrial estates in Borsele. The priority is to use free space in our own industrial estates, and if those are full, start-ups can use available space in surrounding municipalities.

111107.explanation: PvdA/GL believes that entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves whether they want to open their shops on Sundays.

111110.explanation: The green dike landscape is unique in the world. We want not only to preserve it but also to make it more beautiful. Solar panels on roofs have our first priority.

111113.explanation: PvdA-GroenLinks wants to strongly advocate for school swimming to return to physical education.

111115.explanation: Beveland Wonen not only builds good and affordable homes, but they must also be sustainable and gas-free. To give starters a fair chance, we agree in new construction plans that 25 percent of the new homes are intended for them.

111119.explanation: To increase social cohesion in the villages, we invest with residents in strengthening contacts with labor migrants.

111120.explanation: The construction of new nuclear power plants has too great an impact on space, housing, and living environment quality in Borsele. The construction costs are sky-high. We therefore fundamentally reject these plans.

111121.explanation: PvdA-GroenLinks wants building in all 15 villages of Borsele, so everyone has a chance at a nice and affordable home.

111067.explanation: You should look at the quality of people; gender does not matter then.

111068.explanation: The residents have chosen their representatives in the Municipal Council.

111075.explanation: This is also the wish of the sector itself.

111079.explanation: In a municipality with 15 villages, you still serve only a limited portion of the residents.

111080.explanation: The collection of household waste must be cost-covering. If household waste collection were free, all other households would have to pay for it.

111083.explanation: Let's protect the rural area from arbitrariness like in Belgium. Building should be done in the villages.

111084.explanation: This already happens and is a good thing. People who find themselves on social assistance through no fault of their own should not be dumped into social isolation.

111085.explanation: The school buildings in Borsele are of good quality. You also need to consider whether certain schools could be merged.

111088.explanation: We must take good care of our cultural heritage.

111091.explanation: What use is that in 15 small villages?

111092.explanation: Let's leave this to the larger municipalities.

111096.explanation: Let’s work collectively on combating poverty.

111097.explanation: This limit was recently increased and coordinated with the surrounding municipalities.

111099.explanation: But also ensure more parking spaces.

111103.explanation: If the municipality continues to have a surplus annually, this can also be realized.

111106.explanation: Let’s first look at what the needs are and then redevelop the older industrial sites we already have. For example, in Heinkenszand, we can expand by connecting the industrial areas.

111107.explanation: This should be left to the entrepreneur.

111113.explanation: Not feasible in Borsele. The swimming pool is in Goes, meaning you have to take a bus to get there.

111115.explanation: You should build according to need.

111119.explanation: You should spread them out as much as possible to limit any nuisance.

111120.explanation: The VVD still prefers nuclear energy over wind turbines and solar parks on valuable agricultural land.

111121.explanation: For some villages, a street of new homes is sufficient. You need to provide tailor-made solutions and look at what the need is.

111067.explanation: This is preferred, with D66 also wanting to emphasize balance based on diversity. The number of women in the Borsele council is not increasing, and therefore the male/female ratio in the executive board remains behind.

111068.explanation: D66 strongly supports the organization of citizen assemblies on important topics. Constructive citizen participation is preferred over a referendum in our small municipality.

111071.explanation: D66 values the protection of landscape and nature and only wants to allow space for small-scale tourism. 'Always allowing it' does not fit with this policy.

111072.explanation: Barriers for new cooperation for a sustainable future must be given every opportunity and should not get stuck in regulations and obstacles. Additional municipal subsidies could contribute.

111075.explanation: D66 supports housing on private grounds, but this must meet the minimum standards for humane living. The option to house migrant workers off the company premises should be investigated. We will establish clear requirements and conditions for this.

111079.explanation: This can create a festive atmosphere in the villages. Organization must always be in consultation with the residents.

111080.explanation: In 2027, Borsele must establish a new waste policy. Collecting bulky waste could be reintroduced. Costs or no costs in a comprehensive waste plan need to be weighed. The realization of a recycling center at the new environmental site in Goes should also play a role.

111083.explanation: D66 believes multiple homes on private property should be possible. The environmental vision and current environmental plan offer little room for extra housing construction in the countryside. We can relax these rules while respecting the value of our rural area.

111084.explanation: There are various financial arrangements in Borsele for people with low incomes. GR De Bevelanden coordinates this. D66 supports these arrangements.

111085.explanation: Where still applicable. D66 supports the merger of schools and thus also school buildings. This can be investigated in Heinkenszand. D66 sees a 'school cluster Center Heinkenszand' and full-fledged Integrated Child Centers as a new development plan in the heart of the village.

111087.explanation: Sports clubs are volunteer organizations with members. If the number of members drops sharply and the club no longer has a raison d'être, then the club closes. Borsele invests heavily in sports locations and football fields, and in sports and exercise in a broad sense.

111088.explanation: More money should be allocated for this. D66 envisions a fund and organization for 'Borselse icons' for this purpose.

111089.explanation: The library in Heinkenszand is the main library with several smaller sublocations in other villages. The Heinkenszand library should be upgraded and play a central role as the living room of Heinkenszand.

111091.explanation: Only if there are reasons for expansion. Many private doorbells are already a disguised form of camera surveillance. More 'big brother' monitoring should only be expanded if necessary.

111092.explanation: This is arranged jointly at Beveland or Zeeland level.

111094.explanation: Given the current pressure on the housing market for the coming years, allowances should be limited and subject to conditions.

111096.explanation: D66 supports social policies for people in financial difficulties.

111097.explanation: The cost of living continues to rise. The relationship between those costs and incomes is skewed. This needs to be brought more into balance.

111099.explanation: This has already improved significantly in recent years, but it can still get better!

111101.explanation: Care services must function without bias. Having preferences is allowed, but this is no right.

111103.explanation: The market value of homes determines the property tax. Lowering it is not an option; limiting increases is.

111106.explanation: The pressure on the landscape in the small-scale polders and the beautiful landscape of Zak and De Poel is under pressure. Companies that want to expand, we would prefer to relocate to new industrial estates in or outside Borsele.

111110.explanation: We favor the active deployment of sustainable energy. Agricultural land is not preferred, but it should remain possible if no alternative locations are available.

111113.explanation: A contribution towards swimming lesson costs for people with a low income is our preference.

111115.explanation: The need for social housing in Borsele is less than in larger municipalities. The ratio of social housing should be based on local demand. This is determined in close cooperation with Beveland Wonen.

111119.explanation: D66 believes that the workplace should lead housing decisions. A good distance to facilities is desirable but not a requirement.

111120.explanation: D66 aims for a sustainable energy mix, which includes nuclear energy. We have doubts about old-style nuclear power plants and prefer SMR development in Zeeland. There is space for this in Borsele.

111121.explanation: Building according to the style and character of villages, where short- and medium-term space/land is available. Building in the smallest villages based on demonstrable demand and in a small-scale setup.

111067.explanation: A healthy ratio is indeed often 1 to 1, but expertise, passion, and transparency also play a role.

111068.explanation: Good idea, but does the municipal council determine if this is an important subject or are village councils involved? The SP supports the overall picture.

111071.explanation: A limited campsite, for example 6 places, and not near Natura 2000 areas, may be considered.

111072.explanation: Only if it does not cause nuisance.

111075.explanation: It may certainly be requested, but control over reasonable shelter placement must also remain our focus.

111079.explanation: As a municipality, you can ask residents via a referendum whether they agree with a municipal levy for this.

111080.explanation: Twice and only for furniture owned by private individuals.

111083.explanation: In terms of planning and infrastructure.

111084.explanation: Exercise and social contact are important for everyone, including those who cannot afford it financially.

111085.explanation: Only where supported by evidence as necessary.

111088.explanation: A part of our 'being,' our history.

111089.explanation: A larger library is not easily accessible to everyone. However, it does offer more facilities.

111092.explanation: Unfortunately, there is regularly a need for a place to escape domestic violence.

111096.explanation: Support to prevent the poverty trap.

111101.explanation: Care is not tied to religion, culture, or beliefs. Labor market shortages also make this position unfeasible.

111103.explanation: Can be reviewed every three years to see if there is room within the annual budget.

111106.explanation: Neoliberal thinking wants more business parks, which does not fit with the current Zeeland Landscape.

111110.explanation: The SP is somewhat supportive of solar panels but not in rural areas.

111113.explanation: Super important for our children and their safety.

111115.explanation: The shortage of social rental housing is large.

111119.explanation: Being able to integrate into the community is important both for residents and labor migrants.

111120.explanation: There are studies indicating that sufficient electricity is generated by wind and solar energy. However, large storage batteries are needed for days with lower production.

111121.explanation: Depends on input concerning the village.

statements.title: Borsele should oppose the construction of nuclear power plants.

titleParts.text: Borsele moet zich verzetten tegen de komst van

titleParts.glossary: Power plants that generate electricity by splitting the atoms of certain substances.

statements.title: More business parks / industrial sites should be developed in Borsele.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Borsele should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: Each village in Borsele should get a new residential development.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The residents of Borsele should be able to give their opinion on important topics via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Borsele moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
Borsele should have a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
At least 40% of the new homes to be built in Borsele should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Borsele moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be one larger library in Borsele instead of smaller libraries in each village.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Migrant workers should primarily live in the larger villages and on the edges of towns.
